prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene computed at DFT tier 'standard' — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF, density-fit RI), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The neutral, cation (charge +1, mult 2), and anion (charge −1, mult 2) single-point energies were used to compute finite-difference Mulliken Fukui indices (f+, f−, f0) and the dual descriptor (Δf = f+ − f−) for all 14 atoms.

Headline result: most electrophilic site f+ = 0.49641779 at atom 4 (C, ipso carbon bonded to –NO₂). Most nucleophilic site f− = 0.1072882 at atom 5 (C, ortho ring carbon). All integrity checks passed (sum f+ = 1.0000, sum f− = 1.0000); trustworthy = true. No warnings reported. Plot at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/nitrobenzene/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260703-200310_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
